channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: WATCH: Owl flies into Virginia home, lands atop Christmas tree
item.description: One family in Virginia got quite the surprise on Wednesday, Dec. 18, when a barred owl flew into their home through the chimney and ended up perched on their Christmas tree.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Officer fatally shot in a North Carolina supermarket, suspect in custody, police say
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (AP) — A police officer responding to a report of a man with a gun inside a North Carolina supermarket was fatally shot Monday and a suspect was later taken into custody, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Police officer killed in North Carolina grocery store shooting just days before Christmas
item.description: Greensboro Police Officer Michael Horan, 44, was shot and killed inside a Food Lion grocery store just days before Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania police announce arrest of The Grinch in holiday video
item.description: Dressed in his Santa suit and ready to cause some mischief, The Grinch managed to make off with someone's gifts.
item.dc:creator: Drew Miller, NewsNation

item.title: Relief, defiance, anger: Families and advocates react to Biden's death row commutations
item.description: COLUMBIA, South Carolina (AP) — Victims' families and others affected by crimes that resulted in federal death row convictions shared a range of emotions on Monday, from relief to anger, after President Joe Biden commuted dozens of the sentences.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates before Trump can resume executions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday announced that he is commuting the sentences of 37 of the 40 people on federal death row, converting their punishments to life imprisonment just weeks before President-elect Donald Trump, an outspoken proponent of expanding capital punishment, takes of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inside the state and federal charges against the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO
item.description: A triptych of criminal charges paints a searing, sometimes disparate portrait of the man accused of ambushing and killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bill Clinton admitted to hospital with fever
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted to a Washington hospital on Monday with a fever, his spokesperson said on social media.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Judge rules Arkansas law allowing criminal charges against librarians is unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge has struck down key parts of an Arkansas law that would have allowed criminal charges against librarians and booksellers for providing so-called “harmful” materials to minors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Adnan Syed's lawyers seek to reduce his prison sentence
item.description: Attorneys for Adnan Syed, whose legal case was chronicled in the hit podcast “Serial,” are seeking to have his prison sentence reduced.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: VIDEO: Van bursts into flames inside California tunnel
item.description: Emergency crews responded to a vehicle fire inside the Caldecott Tunnel in the San Francisco Bay Area on Monday morning, according to the California Highway Patrol. Eastbound Highway 24 is shut down inside Bore 1 after a minivan caught fire at around 9:54 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Taylor Swift makes $250,000 donation to Kansas City nonprofit
item.description: The global superstar made a $250,000 donation to Operation Breakthrough, a nonprofit that helps families in the city's urban core.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Rousseau, NewsNation

item.title: What are 'snow rollers'? Photos of rare phenomenon in Michigan spark curiosity
item.description: “This is the coolest thing I've ever seen,” one viewer wrote, remarking on the dozens of doughnut-shaped snowballs peppered across a field.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Civil rights icon James Meredith is honored in his Mississippi hometown
item.description: KOSCIUSKO, Miss. (AP) — A new historical marker has been unveiled in the hometown of James Meredith, honoring the Black man who fought white supremacy by integrating the University of Mississippi in 1962.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teen brothers missing after weekend duck-hunting excursion goes horribly wrong
item.description: Two teenage brothers, Wesley and Andruw Cornett, have been missing for over a week in Northern California after they went duck hunting in stormy weather on Dec. 14.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nordstrom to be acquired by Nordstrom family and a Mexican retail group for $6.25B
item.description: ntury-old department store Nordstrom has agreed to be acquired and taken private by Nordstrom family members and a Mexican retail group for $6.25 billion, the company said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Abortion opponents shift focus to pills with lawsuits, proposed laws and possible federal action
item.description: Opponents of abortion are increasingly focusing on restricting access to pills, which are the most common way to end a pregnancy in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 174 Colorado skiers and snowboarders rescued after a lift cracks
item.description: WINTER PARK, Colo. (AP) — Officials were investigating Sunday what caused a crack in a Colorado ski lift that forced the evacuation of over 170 stranded skiers and snowboarders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Virginia woman faces jail time after practicing illegal dentistry
item.description: A Virginia woman has been convicted of practicing dentistry without a license, following accusations that she illegally performed veneer procedures at her tooth jewelry business.
item.dc:creator: Karina F. Garcia, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lindsey Vonn took all of 20 seconds to regain her former speed in comeback race at age 40
item.description: Lindsey Vonn took just a handful of gates this weekend to recover from a poor start and rediscover her former speed when she placed 14th in a super-G.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Louisiana often holds inmates past their release date, DOJ lawsuit claims
item.description: Louisiana’s prison system routinely holds people weeks and months after they have completed their sentences, the U.S. Department of Justice alleged in a lawsuit filed Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What are 'snow rollers'? Photos of rare phenomenon in Michigan spark curiosity
item.description: “This is the coolest thing I've ever seen,” one viewer wrote, remarking on the dozens of doughnut-shaped snowballs peppered across a field.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A rocket from Yemen strikes Tel Aviv, injuring 16, and Palestinians mourn a dozen children in Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — A rocket fired from Yemen hit an area of Tel Aviv overnight, leaving 16 people injured by shattered glass, the Israeli military said Saturday, days after Israeli airstrikes hit Houthi rebels who have been launching missiles in solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB's all-time stolen base leader, dies at 65: reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, ABC 4

item.title: What NYC Mayor Adams told Luigi Mangione when they met in NYC
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he “wanted to send a strong message” when he met accused UnitedHealthcare CEO killer Luigi Mangione in New York City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, KRON4

item.title: Senate confirms 235th Biden judge, surpassing Trump’s record
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) marked the achievement after the Senate confirmed Serena Raquel Murillo to serve as district judge for the Central District of California.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, WGN 9

item.title: Over 600,000 Nuna Rava car seats recalled over possible crash failure
item.description: Nuna Baby Essentials is recalling more than 600,000 of its Rava line of car seats over a harness defect that could cause the restraint system to fail in a crash.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: University of California campuses resolve discrimination complaints stemming from Gaza protests
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education said Friday it has reached an agreement with the University of California system resolving complaints from Jewish and Muslim students of discrimination and harassment during protests last spring over the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana man sentenced to the maximum of 130 years in prison for 2017 killings of 2 teenage girls
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2017 killings of two teenage girls who vanished during a winter hike was sentenced to a maximum of 130 years in prison Friday in the case that’s long cast a shadow over the teens’ small hometown of Delphi. Allen, 52, was convicted on Nov.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former top aide to New York City's mayor pleads not guilty in bribery case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The New York City mayor's former chief adviser, her son and two real estate investors were indicted Thursday on bribery and conspiracy charges that came as the mayor himself fights a separate corruption case.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
